title: Medical and Clinical Laboratory Technologists
description: Perform complex medical laboratory tests for diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of disease. May train or supervise staff.

tasks.text: Analyze samples of biological material for chemical content or reaction.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Analyze laboratory findings to check the accuracy of the results.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct chemical analysis of body fluids, including blood, urine, or spinal fluid, to determine presence of normal or abnormal components.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter data from analysis of medical tests or clinical results into computer for storage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and study blood samples to determine the number of cells, their morphology, or their blood group, blood type, or compatibility for transfusion purposes, using microscopic techniques.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up, clean, and maintain laboratory equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate, calibrate, or maintain equipment used in quantitative or qualitative analysis, such as spectrophotometers, calorimeters, flame photometers, or computer-controlled analyzers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Establish or monitor quality assurance programs or activities to ensure the accuracy of laboratory results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise, train, or direct lab assistants, medical and clinical laboratory technicians or technologists, or other medical laboratory workers engaged in laboratory testing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and prepare specimens and media for cell cultures, using aseptic technique and knowledge of medium components and cell requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Obtain, cut, stain, and mount biological material on slides for microscopic study and diagnosis, following standard laboratory procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide technical information about test results to physicians, family members, or researchers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop, standardize, evaluate, or modify procedures, techniques, or tests used in the analysis of specimens or in medical laboratory experiments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Cultivate, isolate, or assist in identifying microbial organisms or perform various tests on these microorganisms.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Harvest cell cultures at optimum time, based on knowledge of cell cycle differences and culture conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
